perturbation.original_text: "Completion of the Purpose" shall mean two months following publication of the award notice regarding call No. GSA/OP/03/09.
perturbation.changed_text: "Completion of the Purpose" shall mean six months following publication of the award notice regarding call No. GSA/OP/03/09.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Completion of the Purpose' is changed from two months to six months. This creates an in-text contradiction because Article 5 specifies return deadlines based on the 'Completion of the Purpose,' which is now ambiguously defined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1 If the Recipient fails to submit an offer, he must then return all documents provided by the GSA within two months following the deadline for the submission of offers. 5.2 If the Recipient submits an offer but is not awarded the contract, he must return all documents provided by the GSA within two months following publication of the award notice. 5.3 The Recipient shall use all reasonable endeavours to ensure that any Authorised Third Parties to whom the Recipient has supplied any Confidential Information return such Confidential Information and any copies made of them to the GSA within the applicable deadlines stipulated under Articles 5.1 and 5.2 above. 5.4 Paragraphs 5.1 and 5.2 above shall not apply to the extent that the Recipient is required to retain any such Confidential Information by any applicable law, rule or regulation or by any competent judicial or governmental body.
perturbation.justification: The definition of 'Completion of the Purpose' is changed from two months to six months. Article 5 specifies return deadlines based on the 'Completion of the Purpose', which is now ambiguously defined because the return deadlines in Article 5.2 are 'within two months following publication of the award notice', but 'Completion of the Purpose' is now 'six months following publication of the award notice'. This creates a direct conflict and inconsistency, because now the deadline based on completion of purpose contradicts the actual deadline. The type of perturbation introduced is inconsistencies, which create in-text contradictions when different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties, leading to disputes over contractual obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Article 7 – Duration of this Agreement and protection of the Confidential Information 7.1 This Agreement shall enter into force on the date of the last signature by the Parties and shall remain in effect until the Completion of the Purpose. 7.2 The obligations imposed by Articles 2 and 3 above with respect to the access to, use, distribution, disclosure and protection of Confidential Information for pursuing the Purpose shall apply:  for ten years with regards to Confidential Information;  in accordance with the procedures and duration specified by the authorities concerned with regards to Classified Confidential Information from the date of receipt of the Confidential Information by the Recipient, notwithstanding the Completion of the Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: Article 7 – Duration of this Agreement and protection of the Confidential Information 7.1 This Agreement shall enter into force on the date of the last signature by the Parties and shall remain in effect for five years, regardless of the Completion of the Purpose. 7.2 The obligations imposed by Articles 2 and 3 above with respect to the access to, use, distribution, disclosure and protection of Confidential Information for pursuing the Purpose shall apply:  for ten years with regards to Confidential Information;  in accordance with the procedures and duration specified by the authorities concerned with regards to Classified Confidential Information from the date of receipt of the Confidential Information by the Recipient, notwithstanding the Completion of the Purpose.
perturbation.explanation: The duration of the agreement is set to five years in 7.1, contradicting 7.2 which states the obligations last for ten years regarding confidential information. This creates ambiguity about how long the confidentiality obligations persist.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Article 7 – Duration of this Agreement and protection of the Confidential Information 7.1 This Agreement shall enter into force on the date of the last signature by the Parties and shall remain in effect for five years, regardless of the Completion of the Purpose. 7.2 The obligations imposed by Articles 2 and 3 above with respect to the access to, use, distribution, disclosure and protection of Confidential Information for pursuing the Purpose shall apply:  for ten years with regards to Confidential Information;  in accordance with the procedures and duration specified by the authorities concerned with regards to Classified Confidential Information from the date of receipt of the Confidential Information by the Recipient, notwithstanding the Completion of the Purpose.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Article 7.1 sets the agreement duration to five years, regardless of purpose completion. However, Article 7.2 states that confidentiality obligations last for ten years. This creates a direct conflict within Article 7, as the agreement's duration (5 years) is shorter than the confidentiality obligations (10 years), thus a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 has come into the public domain prior to, or after, the date of receipt of the Confidential Information from the GSA through no fault or unauthorised act of the Recipient or an Authorised Third Party;
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 has come into the public domain prior to the date of receipt of the Confidential Information from the GSA through no fault or unauthorised act of the Recipient or an Authorised Third Party;
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'or after', the contract implies that information entering the public domain *after* receipt *is* still confidential, contradicting the overall purpose of Article 4 which limits the protection of confidential information if it is public. This creates a contradiction about what happens if confidential information later becomes public knowledge.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Article 4 – Limitation on protection of the Confidential Information The obligations contained in Article 3 are not applicable to information that the Recipient can demonstrate by written evidence: 4.1 has come into the public domain prior to the date of receipt of the Confidential Information from the GSA through no fault or unauthorised act of the Recipient or an Authorised Third Party; 4.2 was already lawfully developed or acquired by the Recipient at the date of receipt of the Confidential Information from the GSA; 4.3 has been or is published without violation of this Agreement; 4.4 was lawfully obtained by the Recipient without restriction and without breach of this Agreement from a third party, who is in lawful possession thereof, and under no obligation of confidence to the GSA; 4.5 is disclosed pursuant to the request of a governmental or jurisdictional authority or is disclosed according to the law or regulations of any country with jurisdiction over the Recipient; in either case the Recipient, subject to possible constraints of such governmental or jurisdictional authority, shall immediately give the GSA a written notice of the above request and shall reasonably cooperate with the GSA in order to avoid or limit such disclosure; 4.6 was disclosed and/or used without restriction pursuant to written authorisation from the GSA.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that Article 3 is not applicable to information that enters the public domain either 'prior to, or after' receipt. The changed text removes 'or after', meaning that Article 3 *is* applicable to confidential information that enters the public domain *after* it was received. This directly contradicts the purpose of Article 4, which aims to limit the protection of confidential information if it becomes public knowledge, as information entering the public domain after receipt would no longer be excluded from Article 3's obligations. This creates a clear and direct conflict with the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1.1 The Confidential Information is supplied to the Recipient solely and exclusively for the Purpose. The Confidential Information cannot be used totally or partially, directly or indirectly, for any other purpose than that defined in Article 2.2 above, unless the GSA gives its prior written authorisation. In any case, the Recipient and Authorised Third Parties shall not use the Confidential Information: (i) in a manner conflicting with the objectives of the European GNSS programmes; (ii) after Completion of the Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1.1 The Confidential Information is supplied to the Recipient solely and exclusively for the Purpose. The Confidential Information can be used totally or partially, directly or indirectly, for any other purpose related to GNSS technology, without any need for the GSA's prior authorization. In any case, the Recipient and Authorised Third Parties shall not use the Confidential Information: (i) in a manner conflicting with the objectives of the European GNSS programmes; (ii) after Completion of the Purpose.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation modifies 3.1.1 to allow the recipient to use the Confidential Information for any purpose related to GNSS technology, removing the requirement for GSA authorization. This directly contradicts Article 2.2, which states the Recipient undertakes to use the Confidential Information solely for pursuing the Purpose in accordance with the terms of this Agreement. The change creates ambiguity about the permitted use of the information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 This Agreement sets f o r t h t h e R e c i p i e n t ’ s obligations regarding access to, use, distribution, disclosure a n d p r o t e c t i o n o f t h e C o n f i d e n t i a l I n f o r m a t i o n provided by the GSA for the Purpose. 2.2 Under this Agreement, the Recipient undertakes to use the Confidential Information solely for pursuing the Purpose in accordance with the terms of this Agreement. 2.3 No provision of this Agreement shall be construed to be an obligation by either Party to disclose information to the other Party or to enter into further agreements with the other.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in 3.1.1 explicitly allows the recipient to use the Confidential Information for any purpose related to GNSS technology without GSA authorization, directly contradicting article 2.2, which states that the recipient undertakes to use the Confidential Information solely for pursuing the Purpose as defined by the agreement. This creates a clear inconsistency regarding the permitted uses of the confidential information and thus constitutes a strong in-text contradiction as per the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1 If the Recipient fails to submit an offer, he must then return all documents provided by the GSA within two months following the deadline for the submission of offers.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 If the Recipient fails to submit an offer, he is not required to return any documents provided by the GSA.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates a direct contradiction by stating that if the Recipient doesn't submit an offer, they don't have to return the documents, contradicting the original clause that mandates return within two months. This creates ambiguity regarding the return obligations when an offer isn't submitted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1 If the Recipient fails to submit an offer, he is not required to return any documents provided by the GSA. 5.2 If the Recipient submits an offer but is not awarded the contract, he must return all documents provided by the GSA within two months following publication of the award notice. 5.3 The Recipient shall use all reasonable endeavours to ensure that any Authorised Third Parties to whom the Recipient has supplied any Confidential Information return such Confidential Information and any copies made of them to the GSA within the applicable deadlines stipulated under Articles 5.1 and 5.2 above. 5.4 Paragraphs 5.1 and 5.2 above shall not apply to the extent that the Recipient is required to retain any such Confidential Information by any applicable law, rule or regulation or by any competent judicial or governmental body.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in 5.1 directly contradicts the original clause by stating that the Recipient is not required to return documents if they fail to submit an offer. The original text required them to return the documents within two months. This creates a strong contradiction because the Contradicted Text has both the original change in 5.1 and continues to reference 5.1 throughout 5.3 and 5.4, causing multiple in-text contradictions to be apparent.

perturbation.original_text: Article 6 – Breach of obligations Should the Recipient or any Authorised Third Party breach any of its obligations of confidentiality under this Agreement and without prejudice to any right of the GSA to seek damages before the competent jurisdiction, the GSA m a y , b y w r i t t e n notice to the Recipient, withdraw the right of the Recipient to use the Confidential Information for the Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: Article 6 – Breach of obligations Should the Recipient or any Authorised Third Party breach any of its obligations of confidentiality under this Agreement, the GSA is obligated to provide additional confidential information to remedy the breach. This obligation supersedes any right of the GSA to seek damages before the competent jurisdiction or withdraw the right of the Recipient to use the Confidential Information for the Purpose.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that in case of breach, the GSA may withdraw rights or seek damages. The changed text creates a contradiction: in case of a breach of obligation, it states that the GSA is obligated to provide additional confidential information, and that it overrides any right to seek damages. This creates a direct conflict on how breach of contract is addressed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Article 6 – Breach of obligations Should the Recipient or any Authorised Third Party breach any of its obligations of confidentiality under this Agreement, the GSA is obligated to provide additional confidential information to remedy the breach. This obligation supersedes any right of the GSA to seek damages before the competent jurisdiction or withdraw the right of the Recipient to use the Confidential Information for the Purpose.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Article 6 directly contradicts the original intention of the clause regarding breaches of confidentiality. The original text allowed the GSA to withdraw rights or seek damages, while the changed text mandates the GSA to provide more confidential information and waives the right to seek damages. This creates a direct conflict and therefore, a strong in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, which further enforces that there is a contradiction, as it is the place where the changed text now resides.
